etymology.description: semantic 辵(辵) (chuò, "walk; movement") + phonetic 屰(屰) (nì, "go against") -> going in the opposite direction
etymology.interpretation: The character 逆(nì, "reverse") combines 辵(辵) (chuò, "walk; movement") indicating movement with 屰(屰) (nì, "go against") providing the sound and the concept of opposition. This creates the idea of going against the direction of movement, leading to meanings like reverse and contrary.

examples.chinese: 他逆着风前进(他逆著風前進)。
examples.english: He moved forward against the wind.

examples.chinese: 鱼逆流而上(魚逆流而上)。
在困难面前(在困難面前)，他从不逆来顺受(他從不逆來順受)。
examples.pinyin: Zài kùnnán miànqián, tā cóng bù nìlái shùnshòu.
examples.english: In the face of difficulties, he never submits to adversity.
